matches.match_rationale: Campus leaders and high-performing sites accumulate tacit knowledge in meeting notes, reports, and communications, but without curation staff this knowledge is never distilled or shared. AI summarization can automatically condense what high-performing campuses do well — from operations reports, staff feedback, and outcome data — into transferable playbooks that other campuses can absorb quickly. This directly replaces the missing dedicated best-practice curation role.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An automated digest system that ingests campus reports and meeting notes weekly, summarizes high-performing practices by theme, and distributes condensed briefs to campus leaders across the network.

matches.match_rationale: Without a learning platform, campus leaders have no way to query accumulated institutional knowledge — they rely on personal relationships to find out what worked elsewhere. A RAG system over internal documents (curriculum materials, operational SOPs, past improvement plans, staff training resources) enables any campus leader to ask 'how did Campus X solve X problem?' and get a grounded, cited answer from real network experience. This substitutes for ad-hoc peer networking with a scalable knowledge infrastructure.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An internal knowledge Q&A tool where campus leaders can query the network's collective experience — finding precedents, best practices, and documented solutions without needing personal contacts at other campuses.

matches.match_rationale: The absence of dedicated curation staff means best-practice documentation is never written — high-performing sites have no mechanism for converting operational success into transferable content. AI text generation can take structured inputs (outcome data, interview notes, observation summaries) and produce ready-to-share practice guides, case studies, and workshop materials at a fraction of the cost of hiring curriculum writers. This directly addresses the gap in formatted, distributable learning content.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A content generation pipeline that turns campus performance data and leader interviews into formatted practice guides and network newsletters, enabling continuous best-practice publication without dedicated staff.

matches.match_rationale: With no structured mentoring or peer exchange, struggling campuses receive no proactive guidance toward relevant solutions from the network. A recommendation system can match struggling campuses to relevant practices, case studies, or peer mentors based on the specific profile of their challenges — without requiring a human coordinator to make those connections manually. This is a direct structural substitute for the absent formal mentoring and working-group infrastructure.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A network intelligence layer that profiles each campus's performance gaps and automatically surfaces the most relevant practices from high-performing peers, along with suggested peer contacts for deeper exchange.

matches.match_rationale: Campus leaders currently rely on personal relationships to access network knowledge — a high-friction, inequitable mechanism that advantages well-connected leaders. A conversational agent acting as a 'network knowledge coach' could provide every campus leader with on-demand access to institutional knowledge, structured reflection prompts, and guided problem-solving without requiring peer availability. This scales the value of informal peer exchange to all campuses simultaneously.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A network knowledge assistant that campus leaders can consult asynchronously — asking operational questions, surfacing comparable situations from peer campuses, and getting structured guidance drawn from network experience.
